title: TC001-Login success with Remember Me enabled redirects to Home
description: Verifies a user can log in with valid credentials, enable Remember Me, and land on the Home dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the username into the username input (index 153) and submit the form by clicking the submit button (index 177).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[2]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/login' in frame.url
        assert '/home' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Home').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Remember Me checkbox not found on the login page (no interactive element index corresponds to the checkbox).
- Login with 'Remember Me' enabled could not be completed because the control is not accessible on the page.
- Dashboard '/home' was not reached; current URL remains on the login page so the post-login verification could not be performed.

title: TC002-Invalid credentials show an error message
description: Verifies incorrect username/password shows an invalid-credentials error and does not navigate to Home.
code:

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (use exact path http://localhost:4200/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'not_a_real_user_12345' into the username input (index 433). If password input is not present afterwards, report the missing feature and finish the task.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[2]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('not_a_real_user_12345')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/login' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=invalid').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/login' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- ASSERTION: Password input field not found on the /login page; test cannot enter password as required.
- ASSERTION: Unable to submit complete credentials because the login form lacks a password field.
- ASSERTION: 'invalid' error message could not be verified because the login flow could not be completed.

title: TC007-Navigate from Home to Pedidos via sidebar
description: Verifies a logged-in user can open the Orders module by using the Home sidebar navigation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (http://localhost:4200/login) as the test step explicitly requires.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the username/email into the username field (index 435), then submit the form (click index 415).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[2]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        # -> Type the password into the password field (index 444) and click the Login button (index 415).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[3]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        # -> Dismiss the alert dialog by clicking its OK button (index 316) so the app can continue processing the login attempt, then wait for the page to update.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-alert/div[2]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/home' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Sidebar menu').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/pedidos' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed: application displayed alert 'Usuario y/o contraseña incorrectos' after credentials were submitted.
- Dashboard page did not load: URL remained on /login and did not navigate to /home after login attempts.
- Sidebar navigation could not be tested because the user is not authenticated and the Orders module cannot be accessed.

title: TC008-Navigate from Home to Sincronización via sidebar

from playwright import async_api

async def run_test():

title: TC012-Create a new order with at least one item and verify it appears in the orders list view
description: Verifies a user can create an order by selecting products, adding items, saving, and then seeing the new order in the orders list view.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (explicit test step). If navigation fails or page remains blank, report failure.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type 'example@gmail.com' into the username/email field (input index 517).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[2]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        # -> Type 'password123' into the password field (input index 526) and then click the Login/Aceptar submit button (button index 547).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[3]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        # -> Dismiss the authentication error dialog by clicking the alert OK button (index 428), then report test failure because authentication failed and the order-creation flow cannot be executed.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-alert/div[2]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/home' in frame.url
        assert '/pedidos' in frame.url
        assert '/pedido' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"product-search-results") or contains(@id,"product-search-results")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"order-items-list") or contains(@id,"order-items") or contains(@class,"items-list")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"client-selection-list") or contains(@id,"client-selection") or contains(@class,"clients-list")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"payment-terms") or contains(@id,"payment-terms") or contains(@class,"payment-options")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/pedidosLista' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('xpath=//div[contains(@class,"orders-list") or contains(@id,"orders-list") or contains(@class,"pedidos-list")]').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed - error message 'Usuario y/o contraseña incorrectos.' displayed after submitting credentials
- Dashboard/home page did not load - application remained on /login so order creation flow could not be started
- Valid authentication credentials not available; cannot perform order creation or verify orders list

title: TC014-Attempt to save a new order without items and verify validation error is shown
description: Verifies saving an order without adding items shows the 'Add at least one item' validation and stays on the order form.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:4200/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:4200/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Enter the username into the username field (index 145) and submit the login form by clicking the login button (index 177).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[2]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('example@gmail.com')
        
        # -> Type the password into the password field (index 154) and click the login button (index 177), then wait for the app to load.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-router-outlet/app-login/ion-content/div/ion-grid/form/ion-row/ion-col[3]/ion-input/label/div[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        # -> Dismiss the alert by clicking the OK button (index 224), then attempt to log in using the visible ACEPTAR button (index 186), and wait for the app to load so the URL can be checked for '/home'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/app-root/ion-app/ion-alert/div[2]/div[3]/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/home' in frame.url
        assert '/pedidos' in frame.url
        assert '/pedido' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Add at least one item').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        assert '/pedido' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
